name: Momentum
description: Trend-following model focused on range expansion and continuation after compression or pullback absorption.

context: Higher-timeframe impulse or fresh session expansion aligned with trade direction.
Breakout base, recent swing, and nearest liquidity target identified.
Price holding outside the broken range after the initial expansion.
setup: Compression or pullback formed around the breakout level.
Retest rejected without a deep close back inside the prior range.
Consecutive closes maintained in trend direction.
trigger: Break of the retest high for longs or retest low for shorts.
Continuation candle body closed beyond the trigger level when using first-push entry.
Trigger candle size contained within one recent average range.
confirmation: Volume or tape speed increasing through the trigger level.
Reclaimed structure holding as support for longs or resistance for shorts.
invalidation: Two-sided trade returning inside the prior range.
Retest closing back through the breakout base.
Session impulse fading with clear divergence.
noTrade: High-impact news scheduled within the immediate execution window.
Breakout path blocked by nearby higher-timeframe opposing structure.
Spread expansion or wick-dominant trigger candle without close-through.

stopLoss: Protective stop beyond retest low or high, or beyond the breakout base.
Far side of the compression box when structure is loose.
Invalidation distance remaining inside planned tolerance.
takeProfit: First scale at nearby extension or liquidity objective.
Runner valid toward session high, low, or measured move while continuation remains clean.
Target structure fixed before execution with no post-entry widening.
timeExit: Continuation appearing within 3 to 6 execution candles.
Exposure reduced before low-liquidity session handoff.
Scalp breakout closed before dead hours.
earlyExit: Full candle close back inside the prior range.
Counter-impulse candle printing a larger body than the entry candle.
Breakout level failing on the next retest.
